results.title: Today in History, 1964: After Jackie Robinson visit, Concordia’s choir canceled a segregated concert
results.description: On May 30, 1964, a Forum editorial praised Concordia College for canceling segregated choir performances in the South, calling the decision a principled stand against racial discrimination.

results.title: Governor Sindh extends heartfelt congratulations to Nation on Youm-e-Takbeer
results.description: KARACHI - Governor Sindh, Syed Muhammad Nihal Hashmi, has extended heart-felt congratulations to the entire nation on the historic occasion of Youm-e-Takbeer, commemorating Pakistan’s successful nuclear tests conducted on May 28, 1998.

results.title: Fife Highland Cows attraction owner staged ‘dead calf’ protest against shooters
results.description: The owner of Kelty Highland Coos lashed out at shooters next to her land, which she blamed on a cow miscarrying.

results.title: Westminster committee to 'consider' probe into Peter Murrell embezzlement
results.description: Scottish Affairs Committee chair Patricia Ferguson said she was “aware of calls” for an inquiry, after eight of the committee’s members said they backed an investigation
